reason: This finding involves enabling FIPS mode, which is a bootloader and cryptographic policy change. According to policy, FIPS changes are explicitly marked as too_dangerous_to_remediate due to high risks of system unbootable, service failures, and it being installation-time only on preexisting systems.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; System unbootable if kernel parameter misconfigured; Cryptographic policy conflicts causing service failures; Not supported on preexisting systems, leading to permanent findings; Potential for widespread application incompatibility
